items.title: 'No regrets' - Tuchel on England's defeat by Argentina
items.summary: Head coach has no regrets but admits he will be judged Bellingham appears to strike Argentina’s Barco on head Thomas Tuchel shouldered the blame for England becoming far too passive after taking the lead as Argentina ended his team’s World Cup dream with a dramatic late comeback. England were within minutes of a first men’s World Cup final on foreign soil after going ahead through Anthony Gordon early in the second half. But the reigning champions equalised through a pile-driver from Enzo Fernández before the substitute Lautaro Martínez scored the decisive goal in the second minute of injury time to send Argentina through to Sunday’s final against Spain in New York.

items.title: Arsenal forward Trossard joins Besiktas for £15.3m
items.summary: Leandro Trossard has joined Turkish Super Lig side Besiktas in a permanent deal from Arsenal

items.title: Aston Villa sign keeper Ramsey on free transfer
items.summary: Lionesses goalkeeper heading to Villa on a free transfer Millie Turner moves to Birmingham City for £100,000 Aston Villa have signed the former Everton goalkeeper Emily Ramsey on a free transfer. The 25-year-old received a call-up by England in 2023 – as part of the squad that won that year’s invitational Arnold Clark Cup – and in 2021 for a training camp, but is yet to earn a senior cap. Ramsey was a regular in England youth international sides throughout the age groups and is highly admired by Villa.

items.title: World Cup 2026: UK government backs calls for Fifa to investigate Argentina over Falklands banner; Tuchel takes blame amid criticism of tactics – live
items.summary: ⚽ Latest news in aftermath from dramatic day in Atlanta ⚽ Tuchel takes blame | Player guide | Golden Boot | Mail us Thomas Tuchel had already shown this week he’s not someone who is prone to mere pleasantries after a game . The head coach shouldered the blame for England becoming too passive after taking the lead against Argentina, but at the same time said he had “no regrets”. I don’t believe so much in an English thing and a curse or whatever.

items.summary: As England gear up to face Norway at the Miami Stadium for the World Cup quarter-finals, re-live all the goals that got Thomas Tuchel's side there

items.title: How Tuchel's changes spectacularly backfired
items.summary: Thomas Tuchel was England's gambler. A squad few others would have picked. A backs-to-the-wall win over Mexico.

items.title: Experten hyllar Lionel Messi efter nya vändningen: ”Han är regissören”
items.summary: While England shrank away in Atlanta, Argentina’s No 10 was not finished making his mark on the biggest stage To strive, to seek, to find, and not to yield. On a day of ceaseless rolling noise under Atlanta’s vast refrigerated dome, England reached the end of the road, the end of their own capacities at this World Cup, the end of the gears within this team. Mainly they ran into Lionel Messi, who wasn’t ready to be done just yet.

items.title: It was supposed to be different but Argentina showed intent, Tuchel showed fear | Jacob Steinberg
items.summary: England head coach made changes that would have led to Southgate and Eriksson being hammered Lionel Messi has seen buses reverse into position before. England did not confront the greatest player of all time with anything new when they dropped back with a place in the World Cup final within reach. The negativity was lamentable and it was no surprise when punishment arrived in the form of a clinical Argentina fightback .

items.title: In this star-powered World Cup, Spain show value of collective and control | Sid Lowe
items.summary: Luis de la Fuente’s final-bound team have been fuelled by togetherness and a commitment to each other that goes back a long way On the way out of the dressing room in Arlington, Luis de la Fuente gathered his “family” and delivered one last message before the World Cup semi-final against France . He had long known what he was going to say, if not exactly how – it’s what he has been saying for 50 days and more. “I’ll tell them that this is a unique stage, the kind of moment that may never be repeated again, and that we have to be ourselves,” he had suggested 18 hours earlier; now that idea crystallised in a line.

items.title: Unlicensed casinos could face ban on sponsoring UK sports teams from 2027
items.summary: Proposal to protect vulnerable and stop money laundering could affect Premier League clubs and Formula One UK politics live – latest updates Unlicensed casinos could be banned from sponsoring sports teams in the UK from next year, in a move that poses financial questions for Premier League football clubs such as Everton FC and sports including Formula One. Plans for a ban, revealed by the Guardian on Sunday and confirmed by the government on Wednesday, are aimed at protecting vulnerable people and reducing the risk of sport being used by organised crime groups for money laundering.

items.title: Manchester United to target £30m Crysencio Summerville should Marcus Rashford leave
items.summary: West Ham winger available for right price after relegation Rashford has two years remaining on United contract Manchester United will turn their attention towards Crysencio Summerville should Marcus Rashford leave this summer, with the West Ham winger’s potential fee around £30m. Summerville is viewed as having the right profile in regards to a left-sided forward to replace the England international. Since relegation to the Championship, West Ham are open to selling the 24-year-old Dutchman providing they receive a suitable offer.

items.title: How are London City Lionesses able to embark on such a transfer spree?
items.summary: Many are in awe of the investment that Michele Kang is making into the women’s game. Others, though, are baffled There is one team whose transfer activity this summer has captured everyone’s attention. London City Lionesses, a club who finished in the bottom half of the second tier a little over two years ago, have stunned the women’s game by signing a flurry of big names, including the Spain stars Alexia Putellas and Mapi León, the former England goalkeeper Mary Earps, and then, on Wednesday morning, the France winger Kadidiatou Diani.

items.title: Didier Deschamps pays price for breaking free from the shackles that led him to glory | Jonathan Wilson
items.summary: France finally let loose all their attacking power in this tournament and yet when the big test came against Spain they were too open Maybe Didier Deschamps was right all along. He has been criticised over his 14 years in the France job for being too cautious, for prioritising control, for not unleashing his great fleet of attacking players. In this tournament, his last as national manager, Deschamps has relaxed – from a tactical point of view at least; he remains as grouchy as ever in his public utterances.

items.title: ‘I need to prove I belong here’: Andoni Iraola stays humble in new Liverpool era
items.summary: Head coach has no illusions as to the scale of the task and wants to start by reconnecting the team and supporters It was Andoni Iraola’s humility that made the biggest impression at his unveiling as Liverpool’s new head coach on Monday. Without fanfare or bombast the 44-year-old promised not to change as a result of landing the biggest job of his coaching career and also to connect with club and city alike. “The magic of being the Liverpool manager,” as he nicely described the opportunity.

items.title: Julián Álvarez’s extra-time stunner sinks 10-man Switzerland to send Argentina into semi-finals
items.summary: Did anyone think they would do it the easy way? If Argentina are to win this World Cup they will only get there via rollercoaster. A seemingly straightforward night’s work against Switzerland became their tournament in microcosm, threatening to squander it all before finding salvation through a moment of unfettered genius.

items.title: Merino puts Spain into World Cup semi-finals as Belgium rue costly Courtois injury
items.summary: Mikel Merino’s amazing moment arrived once more, an entire country circling the corner flag with him as football, which somehow always finds a way, provided another absurdly tall tale. The man who came on to score the late, late goals that took Spain into a European championship semi-final two years ago and a World Cup quarter-final four days ago, only went and did it again. And this time it was even better, his two-month old son Marco, who he has hardly seen, in attendance here.

items.title: Sam Larsson återvänder till IFK Göteborg: ”En dundervärvning”
items.summary: IFK Göteborg har presenterat ett tungt sommarförvärv. Sam Larsson är tillbaka i Blåvitt efter tolv år utomlands och ansluter omedelbart på fri transfer. Arvid Larsson från SvenskaFans IFK-redaktion har länge hoppats på en återkomst

items.title: Röster efter matchen: "En härlig energi"
items.summary: Det var idel leenden och stor lättnad i den himmelsblå zonen efter 4–0-krossen mot IFK Göteborg. Himmelriket tog tempen på tränare Gaute Hellstrup, debutmålskytten Anton Höög, en iskall Malte Frejd Pålsson och tvåmålsskytten Erik Botheim

items.title: 4 snabba efter Hammarbys seger mot Kalmar
items.summary: Hammarby tog en säker seger med 2-0 mot Kalmar hemma på Nya Söderstadion. Målskyttarna var Paulos Abraham och självmål (efter ett hårt inspel från höger från Montader Madjed). Henrik Rydström tog därmed sin andra raka seger sedan han tog över som ny tränare för Hammarby

items.title: Rydström: ”Hur lojala ska tränare vara?”
items.summary: I sin första hemmamatch som tränare för Hammarby IF ställs Henrik Rydström mot sitt gamla lag Kalmar FF. SvenskaFans träffade Rydström i veckan för ett samtal om historiken med Kalmar, Nanne Bergstrands betydelse för honom och tränarlojalitet

items.title: Panel inför matchen mot Kalmar
items.summary: Vi på S@derstadion tar till ett nygammalt grepp och ersätter den traditionella inför-artikeln med en inför-panel. Det handlar förstås om söndagens match mot Kalmar, där nya tränaren Henrik Rydström får möta det lag som han både spelade i under många år, för att sedan även bli tränare där. De som medverkar i panelen denna gång är Linus Isaksson, Elin Ehrencrona och Oliver Jernelius

items.summary: Ryssland och Belarus hälsas välkomna tillbaka av fler och fler internationella idrottsförbund. Under onsdagen är det handbollen som hälsar nationerna tillbaka in i värmen igen

items.title: Argentina firade med förbjuden banderoll framför fansen
items.summary: Trots förbud om banderoller med politiska budskap firade de argentinska spelarna segern mot England med att veckla ut en banderoll kopplat till Falklandsöarna. Nu väntar straff från Fifa, enligt argentinska uppgifter. – Jag förväntar mig att Fifa gör sin utredning grundligt, säger brittiska näringsministern Peter Kyle

items.title: Kritiken mot Thomas Tuchel efter semiförlusten: ”Ber om problem”
items.summary: England föll mot Argentina i VM-semifinalen. Nu riktas kritiken mot Thomas Tuchels val av byten. – Om man låter Argentina ta över ber man om problem, säger Wayne Rooney till BBC

items.title: Argentina klart för VM-final – efter jättedrama på tilläggstid
items.summary: England hade ena benet i VM-final, men efter två sena mål vände Argentina. – Det här betyder mer för mig än ett mål, mer än en final, säger en tårögd Lautaro Martinez, som skickade Argentina till landets andra raka final med sitt 2–1-mål

items.title: Så blev svenske VM-spelaren Elliot Stroud viral i Argentina
items.summary: Efter en överraskande plats i Sveriges startelva i åttondelsfinalen mot Frankrike lärde sig många nya svenskar Elliot Strouds namn. Men under VM väckte Mjällbyspelaren även medial uppmärksamhet i ett annat land. – Man har tydligen gått och blivit stor i Argentina också, säger Elliot Stroud till SVT Sport

items.title: Pontus Wernbloom om krisen i IFK Göteborg: ”Varit horribelt”
items.summary: IFK Göteborg är i högsta grad indraget i bottenstriden. Ytterligare en gång råder det kris i storklubben. – Jag lägger väldigt mycket av ansvaret det här året på spelarna.

items.title: Gaisfansen om hammarbyaren Nikola Vasic: ”Kunde varit värre”
items.summary: I ett stekhett Göteborg gjorde i dag Gais nyförvärv från Hammarby, Nikola Vasic, sitt första träningspass. – Det kunde ha varit värre, det kunde varit en AIK:are, hördes från fansen

items.title: Jonas Eriksson om heta rivalmötet: ”Det finns så mycket historia”
items.summary: För första gången sedan 2005 ställs Argentina mot England i fotboll. Det klassas av många som världens hetaste rivalmöte, något som SVT Sports expert Jonas Eriksson håller med om. – De viktigaste matcherna har Argentina nästan alltid vunnit, säger Eriksson

items.title: Mer än bara fotboll mellan England och Argentina: ”Infekterad fråga”
items.summary: England och Argentina ställs mot varandra i semifinal i fotbolls-VM i kväll. Ett möte som är större än bara fotboll – speciellt i Argentina. – Det här är en fråga som är infekterad, säger SVT:s Latinamerika-korrespondent Tigran Feiler

items.title: MLS return storylines: Berhalter’s future, Son’s goal drought and what’s next for Messi?
items.summary: The league is back in play after a six-week pause for the World Cup. We spotlight seven players to watch after their stints with their national teams The 2026 MLS season resumes Thursday, cleverly holding its return on the days between the World Cup semi-finals and third-place game. Fans of any of the league’s 30 teams will be trying to recall the state of affairs after a six-week pause, with more than half of the regular season still to be played and the Leagues Cup further congesting the schedule.

items.title: 282: “Blanda om - Ojämna lag”
